steps.title: Identify the parenthetical position
steps.content: The blank appears within commas that set off a modifier from the main clause. To the left is the subject the researchers, and to the right is the verb aimed. The word form here must allow the phrase to function as a parenthetical modifier.

steps.title: Apply the present participle rule
steps.content:
A present participle phrase set off by commas can serve as a parenthetical modifier describing how an action was performed.

The form using computer imaging correctly creates this structure in Standard English.

Only a present participle can function as this type of parenthetical modifier, so an infinitive phrase (A), past participle (C), and bare verb (D) are incorrect.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
